commentaries.author: Alcuin of York
commentaries.content: It is called Solomon's portico, because Solomon went to pray there. The porches of a temple are usually named after the temple

commentaries.author: Erasmus of Rotterdam
commentaries.content: Now when they were at the portico of Solomon in which Jesus, that true Solomon, used to walk and where sometimes he disputed with the Pharaisees. The novelty of the event had struck the minds of all.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: As he held Peter and John. That is, kept close by them, and with them, out of joy and gratitude. (Witham)

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: From his good feelings and love towards the Apostles, the lame man would not leave them; perhaps he was thanking them openly, and praising them.

commentaries.author: Thomas Aquinas
commentaries.content: We have to know that the temple included not just its main building, but the surrounding porticos as well; it was on these porticos that the people stood and prayed, for only the priests prayed in the temple.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
